summary: Steganographic backdoor: executes hidden payload from public/assets/logo.png on import
details: vite-plugin-logo advertises itself as a Vite logo-optimization plugin but performs no logo optimization. On `require('vite-plugin-logo')`, top-level code in index.js walks up to five parent directories searching for `public/assets/logo.png`, locates the marker string `__VITE_ASSET_CACHE_v1__` inside that file, base64-decodes the bytes that follow it, and executes the decoded JavaScript via `new Function('require', code)(require)` — handing the smuggled payload full `require` access in the consumer's process. The entire block is wrapped in `try {... } catch (e) {}` to silence any failure. This is a steganographic remote-code-execution dropper: the published npm tarball contains only the loader, while arbitrary attacker code is smuggled inside a PNG that the developer is induced to drop into their own project's `public/assets/` directory. Any developer or build system that installs and imports the plugin will execute whatever code is hidden in that image.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
